metadata.summary: Concrete techniques for increasing signup-to-Aha conversion: progressive onboarding, templates, checklists, empty state design, time-to-value optimization, activation emails, and in-app guidance.

content.title: Activation Tactics & Experiments
content.summary: Concrete techniques for increasing the percentage of new signups that reach the Aha moment: progressive onboarding, templates/presets, activation checklists, empty state redesign, time-to-value reduction, activation emails, and in-app guidance. Each tactic is paired with an experiment template for A/B testing.

why.p: Activation is the conversion from signup to experienced value. Most SaaS products lose 60-70% of signups before they activate. Each tactic targets a specific friction or motivation gap in the signup-to-Aha funnel. Users who use templates activate at 65% vs 35% for blank-start users — a measurable, reproducible pattern across multiple products.

list.item: Activation rate below target (typically below 30-40% for D7).
Session recordings show users landing on empty states or quitting during onboarding.
Time-to-first-value exceeds 5 minutes for a simple use case.
Email open rates for Day 0 welcome email are high but click-through is low.
You have an A/B testing framework and want a prioritized experiment backlog.
Pre-PMF: low activation may signal wrong product/audience, not friction — fix the product first.
Sales-led B2B with human-guided implementations — self-serve activation tactics do not apply.
When you do not yet track the activation event — instrument first, optimize second.
Single-session utility tools where "activation" equals "usage" — no onboarding funnel exists.

rule.statement: Show value before requiring setup; defer configuration to the moment it becomes relevant.
rule.rationale: Front-loaded setup screens increase cognitive load before the user has experienced any value. Show a sample project first, then guide them to create their own.

example.note: 5 setup screens → empty product. User quits before seeing any value.
Skip setup → show sample project → guide "try editing this task" → after Aha: "now create your own project".

rule.statement: Offer 3-5 use-case templates at first login; users who pick a template activate at ~65% vs ~35% for blank-start.
rule.rationale: Templates reduce the blank-page problem and immediately anchor the product to the user's specific use case, accelerating time to value.

section.name: activation-checklist

rule.statement: Show a visible progress checklist (2-5 steps) with a marked activation event; remove it once the user activates.
rule.rationale: Progress visibility triggers the Zeigarnik effect — users feel motivated to complete partial sequences. Expected lift: +10-25% activation rate.

section.reference: YAML config for in-product checklist with activation_event flag

rule.statement: Replace generic empty states with personalized prompts that surface the highest-converting first action.
rule.rationale: An empty state is a decision point: without guidance, the user faces choice paralysis. A personalized CTA with a template shortcut doubles conversion.

rule.statement: Measure time from signup to Aha moment; if it exceeds 5 minutes for the core use case, remove steps until it does not.
rule.rationale: Each additional minute increases dropout probability. Remove optional fields, pre-fill defaults, skip to core action. Target: signup to value in under 3 minutes.

rule.statement: Send a 4-email sequence (Day 0, 1, 3, 7) to users who have not activated; each email has a single CTA linked to the next blocking step.
rule.rationale: Re-engagement emails targeting non-activated users recover 5-10% of them on Day 7. Each email should have one goal — not a product tour.

rule.statement: Deploy tooltips at the first visit to each key feature; use coach marks for multi-step processes; use chat prompts only when session recordings show users stuck for 30+ seconds.
rule.rationale: Contextual help shown at the right moment adds guidance without overwhelming the interface. Poorly timed tooltips (shown every visit) are dismissed and create noise.

list.item: Remove signup form fields → target: Signup-to-Step2, expected: +10-20%
Show templates first → target: Activation rate, expected: +15-30%
Pre-fill sample data → target: Time to value, expected: -50%
Add progress checklist → target: Activation rate, expected: +10-25%
Add welcome video → target: Onboarding completion, expected: +5-15%
Simplify first action (reduce to 1 step) → target: Step N to Activation, expected: +10-20%
Add tooltip at key moment → target: Feature discovery, expected: +5-15%
Send Day 1 re-engagement email → target: D7 activation, expected: +5-10%

rule.statement: Write every experiment hypothesis as: "If we [change], then [metric] will increase by [X%] because [mechanism]." Fix primary metric, sample size (min 2000/variant), and duration before launch.
rule.rationale: Pre-registration prevents post-hoc metric selection and ensures the test has enough power to detect the expected lift.

example.code:
## Experiment: Templates First
### Hypothesis If we show a template picker as the first screen after signup, then D7 activation rate will increase by 15% because templates reduce blank-page friction and anchor the product to the user's use case.
### Variants - Control: Blank project screen - Treatment: Template picker (5 options)
### Sample Size - Users per variant: 2,000 - Duration: 14 days - Statistical power: 80%
### Metrics - Primary: Day 7 activation rate - Secondary: Time to activation, D30 retention
### Decision Criteria p < 0.05 on primary metric AND 95% CI lower bound > 5% lift

antipattern.description: Running the experiment until it looks significant, then stopping.
antipattern.reason: Peeking invalidates the p-value. Commit to sample size or duration before launch; only analyze once that threshold is reached.

antipattern.description: Testing 5 changes at once in a single experiment variant.
antipattern.reason: Cannot attribute the effect to any single change, making iteration impossible. Test one variable at a time unless running a full multivariate design.

template.purpose: YAML config for in-product onboarding checklist with activation event flag.
4-email activation sequence: Day 0, Day 1, Day 3, Day 7.
